slides.title: ICN+SCION: A global name-based network service
ICN-Enabled Adaptive In-Network Computation for Resilient Distributed AI
From Map-and-Encap to BIER: Observations on Network Routing Scalability
Architecting A Scalable Routing System for Named Data Networking
Open-source decentralized access control library for NDN

chat.author: Junxiao Shi
chat.text:
Prefix aggregation in IP is dying.
Most BGP announcements today are /24 prefixes.
If you own a /24, you can change providers without renumbering your network.

chat.author: David Oran
chat.text: yes - good point.

chat.author: Stuart Card
chat.text: Host Identity Protocol is also map-encap but does not require Internet wide change: it can be adopted incrementally & immediately brings benefits to adopters.

chat.author: David Oran
chat.text: The motivation for MPLS wasn't routing scalability but rather traffic engineering

chat.author: Ryo Yanagida
chat.text: I classified HIP as uh SHIM layer approach in my thesis if I remember correctly

chat.author: David Oran
chat.text: @Stuart - yes, another good example which also tried to solve the host authenticity problem by using key hashes as the identifiers

chat.author: David Oran
chat.text: Of course the question remain of why use OP multicast in the first place rathe than a layer 4 or 7 approach like MOQ

chat.author: Stuart Card
chat.text: @Ryo I just found your thesis, thanks!

chat.author: Roland Bless
chat.text: There are scalable routing schemes that do not require a mapping system. KIRA is a scalable routing system that even does not need an ID->LOC mapping system.

chat.author: David Oran
chat.text: forside 13 - it seems SCION nails spoliations to most of the problems for interdomain scalability across AS's

chat.author: Ryo Yanagida
chat.text: @Stuart Card hmm i think it should still be not public... (and perhaps not so relevant in this context :sweat_smile: )

chat.author: Stuart Card
chat.text: We are seeing adoption & deployment of Drone Remote Identification Protocol (DRIP) based on Hierarchical Host Identity Tags (HHITs) adapted from HIP. I am still working to generalize it to multicast & CIDR.

chat.author: David Oran
chat.text: Q: Do consumers as well as producers need to register with the mapping system? if so, how does that square with the (desirable) consumer anonymity property of NDN?

chat.author: Junxiao Shi
chat.text:
Only the producers need to register with the mapping system.
However, it's unclear how this interacts with Reflexive Forwarding.

chat.author: David Oran
chat.text: @Junxiao - thanks

chat.author: David Oran
chat.text: @Junxiao - I suspect one could construct PIT tokens for the reverse tunnel to get to the right ingress router without the consumers to have public identities.

chat.author: Tianyuan Yu
chat.text: Yes consumers do not need to register prefix. The prefixes are for the producers.

chat.author: Kenneth Calvert
chat.text: @Tianyuan Yu, does there need to be a PET entry for every sync application instance (i.e., multicast group)?

chat.author: Tianyuan Yu
chat.text: @Ken, Yes you need to by design. We are working on some on-demand mechanisms so that routers dynamically write popular prefixes into the PET.

chat.author: Kenneth Calvert
chat.text: @Tianyuan, thanks.

chat.author: Junxiao Shi
chat.text:
The risk of ABE and ABAC is the lack of well-maintained libraries.

NAC-ABE is fighting with OpenABE that is incompatible with OpenSSL 3.
ABAC-NDN is using PBC library that still works for now, but PBC is not actively maintained either.

chat.author: Lixia Zhang
chat.text: this NDN TR seems relevant to this talk (regarading a layer between app and net, see https://named-data.net/wp-content/uploads/2021/01/NDN-TR-0071-ndn-lite-pubsub.pdf

chat.author: Lixia Zhang
chat.text: the issue of prodiucer and consumer belonging to different trust domains is resolved by inter-domain trust management, see an early poster at ICN 2022, https://dl.acm.org/doi/pdf/10.1145/3517212.3559489

chat.author: Lixia Zhang
chat.text: Ownly adopted IETF standard MLS in the latest Ownly release

chat.author: Tianyuan Yu
chat.text: dev.ownly.work

chat.author: Tianyuan Yu
chat.text: https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/rfc9420/

chat.author: Lixia Zhang
chat.text:
As I said earlier, let's keep the communication channel open and update each other on the latest work.  We still run weekly NDN coordination calls that are open to everyone, where we discuss/debate new changes (just join nfd-dev mailing list to receive call agenda every Thursday).  
But it is our fault for not reporting NDN updates to the ndn-interest mailing list. We will fix that.

chat.author: David Oran
chat.text: @lixia - it may be valuable for a subset of things that may have wider interest to occasionally post to th eICNRG list as the membership only partially overlaps

chat.author: Lixia Zhang
chat.text: yes we can do that.
